data.body:
### [6/10] DOSSIER A — Snapshot substrate: WHY the reconciler is snapshot-based

DISAMBIGUATION (load-bearing): "snapshot" names THREE unrelated subsystems. (A) Jira reconciler snapshots prev/curr — governed by ADR 0004 only. (B) code-reading gate cache (ADR 0005) = content-addressed git-tree cache for LLM gates — UNRELATED. (C) event-store compaction SNAPSHOT events + replay horizon (ADR 0035) — UNRELATED (the reconciler has NO replay/fold-horizon; it diffs prev-vs-curr). => "prev == a past curr for replay/horizon-safety" is a false import; ADR 0035/0005 do NOT constrain narrowing prev.

WHY SNAPSHOTS AT ALL (not live-per-decision, not a diff log): the ACLI/JQL ceiling — fetcher hits a hard 1000->1200-issue per-query cap with SilentTruncationError (fetcher.py:84-106, _ACLI_CEILING=1200); ~1050 active + ~1120 Done on DIG. Live-per-decision = thousands of round-trips/pass. A diff log can't answer "is this key still present?" which the edge-trigger + census + level-triggered adoption all require (full membership). Snapshot is idempotent/testable (byte-identical on identical data).

WHY prev IS COMMITTED (durable), not in-memory/local: GHA runners are EPHEMERAL — fresh FS every run. Earlier code wrote prev to the main-branch worktree (dies with the run) so prev was always {} and the differ re-derived ALL 2050+ inbound_create mutations every pass (reconcile.py:662-669 verbatim). Fix: write prev under .tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/ so the commit-back step rides it on the tickets branch across runs (reconcile.py:670-673; reconcile-bridge.yml:270-276). Corollary I-FAILCLOSED: a corrupt/conflict-marked prev ABORTS the pass (reconcile.py:678-680, :725-734) because a conflict marker means the tickets branch may be partially merged so curr is also untrustworthy.

PRODUCER<->CONSUMER CONTRACT (ADR 0004, keystone story 8937-eed3, epic f89d): forced by THREE prod bugs of one CLASS where each half was unit-tested against snapshot dicts the real fetcher never emits — 0ee6 (inbound read flat `comments` while fetcher wrote nested `comment`), 3f04 (snapshot never carried `issuelinks` -> link sync structurally dead). Fix: _snapshot_schema.py SSOT (SnapshotEntry TypedDict + JSON Schema) + a STRUCTURE/semantic round-trip test through the production deserialization path (never values/call-counts). SCOPE (critical): validate_snapshot_entry runs ONLY in the contract test, governs the PRODUCER output = CURR, and is NEVER run against prev. So narrowing prev does not violate ADR 0004.

WHY the fetcher stores the FULL payload (not a projection): schema leaves additionalProperties OPEN ("a real issue carries many fields the reconciler doesn't read") — a projection would need widening every time a differ reads a new field, re-opening the 3f04 silently-absent-field failure. Enrichment merges (comment/issuelinks) amortize per-ticket ACLI into one paged REST sweep.

INVARIANTS: I-SCHEMA/I-ROUNDTRIP (curr only); I-DURABLE (prev survives ephemeral runs); I-FAILCLOSED (unparseable prev aborts); I-ANCESTOR (ADR 0026: MUST retain a 3-way-merge baseline for the 5 inbound-mirrored fields; "removing prev_snapshot WITHOUT a replacement last-synced store re-opens 16870dbec and is explicitly rejected"; per-binding baseline is the authorized replacement but "binding-presence is NOT a substitute for the baseline VALUE").

BEARING: narrowing prev to a key-set does NOT violate ADR 0004 (governs curr) nor 0005/0035 (other subsystems). The binding-store baseline already satisfies I-ANCESTOR for the primary consumer. The ONE un-cleared fence = differ._compute_mutations_emit_both (see [4]). Debug artifact (b): keep OUT of the .tickets-tracker commit-back path unless intended on the tickets branch; must NOT be fail-closed (a corrupt debug snapshot must never abort a pass).
Refs: ADR 0004, 0026; reconcile.py:662-768; fetcher.py:84-106,413-527; _snapshot_schema.py; differ.py:263-607; conflict_resolver.py:28-49; run_differs.py:190-268,456-467; commits b534f47a7 (extraction), ea79ecca7 (PR20 GHA topology), 16870dbec (REB-532/ADR0026).
